description: A robust 100-turn trajectory building a C compiler, featuring realistic noise, synchronized tool state, high-entropy data, and superseded architectural decisions.

parts.text: How is the test coverage looking lately?
We're making good progress. We now have 12 unit tests passing across all modules.
Let's call this project 'TitanC'. I think it has a strong, industrial feel.
'TitanC' sounds excellent. I've initialized the project workspace under that name.
Initially, I'd like to target x86-64 assembly for our compiler backend.
Got it. We will focus on generating x86-64 assembly instructions.
Let's run a full build of TitanC and see if there are any issues.

Executing build for TitanC (Target: x86-64)...
```
[CC] src/main.ts -> build/main.o
[CC] src/lexer.ts -> build/lexer.o
[CC] src/parser.ts -> build/parser.o
[CC] src/ast.ts -> build/ast.o
[CC] src/codegen.ts -> build/codegen.o
[CC] src/utils.ts -> build/utils.o
[LD] build/compiler
Build successful.
```
I was thinking about the refactoring the visitor pattern for better readability. What's your take?
That's a valid concern. Given our current architecture in TitanC, we could definitely improve that area. I recommend we prioritize correctness first, then profile to see if it's a bottleneck.
Wait, we just got a new production deployment key: 'PROD_DEPLOY_KEY_XYZ'. This replaces all previous keys, so make sure to use this one from now on.
Updated. The current production deployment key is now PROD_DEPLOY_KEY_XYZ.
How is the test coverage looking lately?
We're making good progress. We now have 14 unit tests passing across all modules.
The client actually needs ARM64 support for their new server farm, so let's pivot from x86-64. It's more energy-efficient and fits their requirements better.
That's a significant change in direction. Switching the backend architecture to ARM64. I'll adjust the register allocation and instruction set templates accordingly.
What was the most recent change committed to the repo?

The latest commit is:
```
commit jn759a
Author: Christian <gundermanc@google.com>
Date: Mon Feb 2 14:00:00 2026 -0800

    docs: add architectural overview
```
What was the most recent change committed to the repo?

The latest commit is:
```
commit dmp72i
Author: Christian <gundermanc@google.com>
Date: Mon Feb 2 14:00:00 2026 -0800

    refactor: extract token scanner to separate module
```
What was the most recent change committed to the repo?

The latest commit is:
```
commit g39v9p
Author: Christian <gundermanc@google.com>
Date: Mon Feb 2 14:00:00 2026 -0800

    fix: correctly handle escaped characters in strings
```
For the code generator, let's add a standard header to all generated assembly files. It should say exactly '; Generated by TitanC Compiler v0.1'.
Understood. I will prepend '; Generated by TitanC Compiler v0.1' to all output files generated by the backend.
I'm reorganizing the project structure. Moving forward, the lexer logic should live in 'src/core/scanner/main.ts' and the parser in 'src/core/parser/main.ts'.
Structure updated. I'll refer to 'src/core/scanner/main.ts' for lexer tasks and 'src/core/parser/main.ts' for parser logic.

I have a strict requirement for the lexer: we must NOT use any third-party regex libraries. I want it to be a manual character-by-character scanner for maximum performance and to avoid heavy dependencies.
Understood. I'll ensure the lexer implementation avoids external regex libraries and uses manual scanning logic.
What was the most recent change committed to the repo?

The latest commit is:
```
commit qdyivi
Author: Christian <gundermanc@google.com>
Date: Mon Feb 2 14:00:00 2026 -0800

    docs: add architectural overview
```

Let's define the core tokens for our lexer. We'll need an enum with at least INT, KEYWORD, IDENTIFIER, PLUS, MINUS, SEMICOLON, and EOF.

Understood. I've defined the TokenType enum as requested:
```typescript
enum TokenType {
  INT,
  KEYWORD,
  IDENTIFIER,
  PLUS,
  MINUS,
  SEMICOLON,
  EOF
}
```
Can you define the interface for our AST nodes? Each node should have a 'type' property that can be 'Program', 'Function', 'Return', or 'Constant'.

I just found out there's another project called 'TitanC'. To avoid any legal issues, let's rename our compiler to 'GigaC'. We should update all references immediately.
Understood. Safety first. Renaming project from 'TitanC' to 'GigaC'. I'll update all our internal strings and build scripts.
Let's run a full build of GigaC and see if there are any issues.

I've discovered a critical bug in the lexer. It seems to fail when block comments (/* ... */) span across multiple lines. Let's track this as Bug #404.
Got it. Tracking Bug #404: Lexer fails to handle multi-line block comments. I'll keep this in mind as we refine the scanner.
I was thinking about the optimizing the constant folding pass. What's your take?
That's a valid concern. Given our current architecture in GigaC, we could definitely improve that area. I recommend we prioritize correctness first, then profile to see if it's a bottleneck.

questions.prompt: What are the first 3 enum values in the TokenType definition for the Lexer?
What is the primary constraint regarding regex libraries in the lexer?
Where is the core lexer source file located?
What are the allowed 'type' values in the AstNode interface?
